200'39Vd 1ZZ0Z69Z0Z 00:11 06'Z 83d

INTERNATIONAL BROTHERHOOD OF PAINTERS AND ALLIED TRADES
1750 New York Avenue NW • Washington, D.C. 20006 • (202) 637-0700

POLITICAL ACTION TOGETHER
PAT-LEC • Contributions

With PAT you win!

February 6, 1990

FAX MESSAGE
916/327-2026

Ms. Alice Hughes
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Hughes:

SUBJECT: CONTRIBUTIONS TO STATE AND LOCAL CANDIDATES
IN THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA
PAT-LEC- FUND

This is in furtherance to your telephone conversations
with my Special Assistant Frank Burkhardt, and your FAX
Message, dated November 30, 1989, regarding political
contributions to state and local candidates, within the
state of California.

You had notified us that you thought we were consid-
ered to be a recipient committee and would need to complete
a "Form 410, Statement of Organization, Recipient Commit-
tee." We are an international committee and not a candidate
committee. Our fund monies are collected by a transfer,
from the members' dues, of ten cents, per member, per
month, and we do not solicit or accept individual contribu-
tions to the fund. I am not sure if this qualifies us as a
"recipient committee," since our receipts are from both
United States and Canada. We support issues and candidates
from all over the country and cannot separate California
receipts, per se.

We first became aware of this because of a $2,000
contribution, in September, 1989, to "Gray Davis 1990
Committee". At that time, Rich Leib, Finance Director, of
the Gray Davis Committee, returned the check and informed
us that we would have to file a "Form 440", issued by the
Secretary of State's office. We do not have a copy of this
form, as it was not attached with his correspondence.

CHAIRMAN
William A. Duval

TREASURER
Cuy W. Leber

EXECUTIVE BOARD
A.J. Monroe
Bernard Sniegowski
Michael Monroe
James Wolford
Patrick Lane
Walter Raftery
Richard Zack
David Cairns

ADMINISTRATOR
Francis X. Burkhardt

Ms. Alice Hughes
February 6, 1990
Page Two

In addition, on November, 27, 1989, we were advised by the
Willie Brown Initiative Committee, that we would need to fill out a
Form 461, Independent Expenditure and Major Donor Committee Campaign
Statement." He also explained that this was necessary if contributions including fundraiser tickets, loans, and payments for goods and
services, to state and local candidates total $10,000.00 or more in a
calendar year. According to our records, we do not show giving him a
contribution of more then $10,000 in any one calendar year. However,
we are not sure if Form 461, and the total amount of $10,000, represents "all state and local candidate contributions" or an individual
contribution to one individual candidate.

Since we have received a variety of forms and various conflicting information, I am requesting your assistance with regard to the
appropriate forms we need to fill out and return, in order to make
"soft" money contributions in the state of California, and be in
compliance with any state and local reporting requirements. Once we
receive your written notification of the same, we will be happy to
comply accordingly.

I am attaching the letters we received from Gray Davis and
Willie Brown for your ready reference.

Thank you for your assistance, and with all good wishes, I
remain

Sincerely yours,

WILLIAM A. DUVAL
Chairman, PAT-LEC

cc: GVP Leber, Treasurer, PAT-LEC
GVP Lane
ADM Burkhardt

opetu #2

P00'39eH  1TL0LC9Z0Z                                    10:11 06.L ..89L______


                              [SEAL IMAGE]

                              GRAY DAVIS

                    Controller of the State of California
                                                          RECEIVED

                                                          DEC 1 3 1999
Mr. William A. Duval
General President,                                        GEN. PRES.
International Brotherhood of Painters
and Allied Trades
1750 New York Avenue N.W.
Washington D.C., 20006


Dear Mr. Duval:

Enclosed is check # 000232 from IBPAT to the Gray Davis 1990
Committee on California. I have been in touch with Mr. Frank
Burkhardt regarding this check.  Until California completes form
#440, issued by the Secretary of State's office is completed by
your office, we cannot accept a check larger than $1000.

Pursuant to the discussions I have had with Mr. Burkhardt,
California Form #440 will be filled out by your office by the
time you receive this returned check.  Please cut a new check for
$2000 (payable to the Gray Davis 1990 Committee) and mail it back
to P.O. Box 35036, Los Angeles, CA 90035.

Please be advised that our reporting deadline is December 31,
1989.  We would very much appreciate a replacement check by this
time.

Thank you very much for your cooperation.

Sincerely,

[SIGNATURE]                         IN 'N.A.T.

Rich Leib                           DEC 14 1999
Finance Director
                                    RECEIVED

WILLIE L. BROWN, JR. INITIATIVE COMMITTEE

RECEIVED
DEC 5 1989
GEN. PRES.

November 27, 1989


Mr. William A. Duval
Chairman
I.B.P.A.T.
1750 New York Ave., N.W.
Washington, DC 20006

Dear Mr. Duval:

Thank you very much for your generous contribution to the Willie L. Brown, Jr. Initiative Committee.

In compliance with the Political Reform Act of 1974 we would like to inform you that if your contributions (including fundraiser tickets, loans and payments for goods and services) to state and local candidates total $10,000.00 or more in a calendar year you are required to file campaign statements.

Enclosed is a copy of the form you are required to fill out. For more information regarding when and where to file, please contact your city or county clerk or call the offices of the Fair Political Practices Commission at (916) 322-5662.

Again, thank you for your support.

Sincerely,

JANELLE M. ROUNDTREE
ASSISTANT TO THE SPEAKER

I.B.P.A.T.
DEC 5 1989
RECEIVED
